economic_notes: GBEs include LBB Gas Bar & Convenience LP/Ltd., LBB Coffee Franchise LP/Ltd., Wakayos Developments LP/Ltd., Wakayos Holdings Inc., URSA Brands Inc., Wakayos Employment & Training Center Inc., and LBB Orchards LP/Ltd. Multiple business ventures including gas bar, coffee franchise, developments, orchards, and branding. Community/Health Centre under construction financed by PHT Term Loan #9 ($2.1M approved). Water truck acquired via finance lease.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Net debt position of $8.39M, up significantly from $3.81M prior year, driven by large capital construction (assets under construction $3.5M) and new PHT Term Loan #11 of $2.5M. Six GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis showing combined reserve for losses of $1.34M. Accumulated deficit of $942K vs prior year surplus of $1.26M — a swing of over $2.2M. Unexpended funds deferred revenue jumped from $90K to $764K. COVID-19 special needs funding received and deferred. Annual debt service calculated as CMHC repayment $16,645 + long-term debt repayment $1,356,276 = $1,372,921. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $3,081,576 + FNIHB $572,417 + CMHC subsidy $12,336 + File Hills Health $6,167 + FHQ Tribal Council $226,707 + YTC CFS $208,925 (partially government-sourced) - classified conservatively. OSR includes land leases, trust funds, dividends, other revenue. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text.
